input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's a complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS targeting iOS and Android.

---

## 1. Prerequisites

### Xcode (for iOS)
- **Source:** Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/)
- Install Xcode first — it's the largest download (~12 GB+)
- After install, run: `xcode-select --install` (installs CLI tools)
- Accept the license: `sudo xcodebuild -license accept`
- Open Xcode once to install any additional components it prompts for
- **Version discovery:** Check [Microsoft's MAUI support policy](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/en-us/platform/support/policy/maui) for the Xcode version required by your chosen .NET version

### Android Studio (for Android)
- **Source:** [developer.android.com/studio](https://developer.android.com/studio)
- During install, accept the Android SDK licenses
- After install, open **SDK Manager** → install:
  - Android SDK Platform (latest stable API level)
  - Android SDK Build-Tools
  - Android SDK Command-line Tools
  - Android Emulator + a system image for testing
- Set env vars (add to `~/.zshrc`):
  ```bash
  export ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk"
  export PATH="$PATH:$ANDROID_HOME/platform-tools:$ANDROID_HOME/cmdline-tools/latest/bin"
  ```

---

## 2. Install .NET SDK

- **Source:** [dot.net/download](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/en-us/download)
- Download the latest **.NET SDK** (not just the runtime) for macOS — pick **Arm64** for Apple Silicon or **x64** for Intel
- **Version discovery:** The MAUI workload ships with the SDK. Check [MAUI release notes](https://github.com/dotnet/maui/releases) to see which .NET SDK version is required/recommended
- Verify: `dotnet --version`

---

## 3. Install the MAUI Workload

```bash
sudo dotnet workload install maui
```

This pulls in all sub-workloads: `maui-ios`, `maui-android`, `maccatalyst`, etc.

- **Verify installed workloads:**
  ```bash
  dotnet workload list
  ```
- **Check available versions:**
  ```bash
  dotnet workload search maui
  ```

---

## 4. Accept Android SDK Licenses

```bash
$ANDROID_HOME/cmdline-tools/latest/bin/sdkmanager --licenses
```

Accept all licenses (type `y` for each prompt).

---

## 5. (Optional) Install an IDE

| IDE | Source |
|-----|--------|
| **Visual Studio Code** + C# Dev Kit extension | [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com) |
| **JetBrains Rider** | [jetbrains.com/rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/) |

> Visual Studio for Mac has been retired. VS Code with C# Dev Kit or Rider are the recommended IDEs.

---

## 6. Verify Everything Works

```bash
# 1. Check .NET SDK
dotnet --info

# 2. Confirm MAUI workload is installed
dotnet workload list          # should show "maui"

# 3. Create a test project
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# 4. Build for each target
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios          # requires Xcode
dotnet build -f net9.0-android      # requires Android SDK

# 5. Run on Android emulator (start one first via Android Studio)
dotnet run -f net9.0-android

# 6. Run on iOS simulator
dotnet run -f net9.0-ios
```

Replace `net9.0` with your actual TFM (e.g. `net8.0`).

---

## 7. Troubleshooting Checklist

| Symptom | Fix |
|---------|-----|
| `xcode-select: error` | `xcode-select --install` or point to Xcode: `sudo xcode-select -s /Applications/Xcode.app` |
| Android SDK not found | Verify `$ANDROID_HOME` is set and points to the SDK |
| `workload maui not installed` | `sudo dotnet workload install maui` (use `sudo` on macOS) |
| iOS build fails with provisioning error | Open Xcode, sign in with Apple ID under **Settings → Accounts**, enable automatic signing |
| Emulator won't start | Ensure hardware acceleration is enabled; on Apple Silicon it's native |

---

**Install order matters:** Xcode → Android Studio → .NET SDK → MAUI workload. This ensures the MAUI installer auto-detects your platform toolchains.
